items.title: Final: Sparks 89, Fire 72. Los Angeles allowed their fewest points all season, holding Portland to 36% shooting and forcing 17 turnovers. This was the best the defense has looked, especially the second-half effort, and it paid off. Sparks are 5-6. They now go on the road.
items.author: Justin Russo
items.body_excerpt: Final: Sparks 89, Fire 72. Los Angeles allowed their fewest points all season, holding Portland to 36% shooting and forcing 17 turnovers. This was the best the defense has looked, especially the second-half effort, and it paid off. Sparks are 5-6. They now go on the road.

items.title: The Sparks beat the Fire 89-72, winning and earning their first Commissioner's Cup victory. This was easily their best defensive performance of the year. LA forced nine turnovers and held Portland to just 72 points, the lowest point total for a Sparks opponent this year.
items.author: Edwin Garcia
items.body_excerpt: The Sparks beat the Fire 89-72, winning and earning their first Commissioner's Cup victory. This was easily their best defensive performance of the year. LA forced nine turnovers and held Portland to just 72 points, the lowest point total for a Sparks opponent this year.

items.title: Asked about the Sky’s half-court offense following today’s 85-68 loss to Toronto, Skylar Diggins deferred to Tyler Marsh:
items.author: Drew
items.body_excerpt:
Asked about the Sky’s half-court offense following today’s 85-68 loss to Toronto, Skylar Diggins deferred to Tyler Marsh:

“That’s a Tyler question. Ask Tyler.”

Later, Marsh said he and Diggins are “on the same page.”

items.title: The Fire have gone scoreless for the last two minutes. The Sparks now lead 75-57 with under six minutes left to play.
items.author: Edwin Garcia
items.body_excerpt: The Fire have gone scoreless for the last two minutes. The Sparks now lead 75-57 with under six minutes left to play.

items.title: Sparks have opened up a 71-57 lead with roughly 7 minutes left in the fourth quarter, thanks in large part to a swarming defense that has forced 12 second-half turnovers and held Portland to 35% shooting on the night. Fire are 2-for-22 on 3s.
items.author: Justin Russo
items.body_excerpt: Sparks have opened up a 71-57 lead with roughly 7 minutes left in the fourth quarter, thanks in large part to a swarming defense that has forced 12 second-half turnovers and held Portland to 35% shooting on the night. Fire are 2-for-22 on 3s.

items.title: Sparks lead Fire 64-55 after three. That third quarter was probably the best 10-minute stretch of defense Los Angeles has played all season, holding Portland to 5-for-14 shooting and forcing 9 turnovers. Kelsey Plum has 12 points, 5 rebounds, and 3 assists.

items.title: Two games. Two thrillers.
items.author: NBA
items.body_excerpt:
Two games. Two thrillers.

Brunson and the Knicks continued their historic run, taking both games in San Antonio. Wemby and the Spurs look to respond as the series shifts to New York.

The NBA Finals continue Monday with SAS (0-2) NYK Game 3 at 8:30pm/et on ABC and ESPN 🏆

items.title: Heading into Game 3 in New York, the Spurs are not only fighting the red-hot Knicks and a daunting dash of NBA history.
items.author: Jeff McDonald
items.body_excerpt:
Heading into Game 3 in New York, the Spurs are not only fighting the red-hot Knicks and a daunting dash of NBA history.

They are also battling a city of 8.5 million ready to party like it's 1973.

"I'd love to spoil their plans," one Spur said.

www.expressnews.com/sports/spurs... via @expressnews
